dhammasaṅgīti.meaning: communal recitation of the Dhamma; rehearsal of the texts; literally, chanting together
breakdown: Chanting together, communal recitation (saṁ, together + √gā/gī, to sing).
dhammasaṅgīti.goenka: Dhammasaṅgīti is literally "chanting together", a rehearsal of the texts: the procedure as traditionally described is that an eminent Thera recites the texts first and then the whole assembly repeats them after him. This is the method by which the word of the Buddha was preserved at the great councils.

dhammatā.meaning: inherent necessity; natural law; the nature of things
breakdown: Nature, law, the way things are (√dhar, to uphold).
Abstract noun suffix: the state of being so, inherent necessity.

dhammavinayassa.meaning: of the teaching and discipline; of the Dhamma and Vinaya

dhammaṭṭhapaṭisaṁyuttā.meaning: connected with the Dhamma and its profitable meaning
breakdown: Meaning, profit, benefit. Sandhi: dhamma + aṭṭha > dhammaṭṭha.
Connected with, associated with (paṭi + saṁ + yutta).

dhamme.gram: masculine noun, locative singular of dhamma (√dhar)
breakdown: The Dhamma, the teaching; the law of nature (√dhar, to uphold).
Locative singular ending: in, with regard to.

dhammikaupāsakopi.meaning: also the lay disciple who follows the Dhamma
dhammikaupāsakopi.gram: masculine compound noun + enclitic particle, nominative singular

dhammoti.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular + quotation particle ti
breakdown: Quotation marker (iti), fused by sandhi: dhammo + ti.

dhammādhipateyyo.meaning: having the Dhamma as master; ruled by the Dhamma
breakdown: Mastery, rulership, supremacy (from adhipati, master, chief). Sandhi: dhamma + ādhi- > dhammādhi-.

dhammānudhammappaṭipanno.meaning: practising in accordance with the Dhamma; following the Dhamma in its entirety, step by step
dhammānudhammappaṭipanno.gram: compound adjective (past participle), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: In accordance with the Dhamma (anu-, along, according to). Sandhi: dhamma + anu- > dhammānu-.
Entered upon, practising, following (past participle of paṭipajjati; the doubled pp is sandhi doubling).

dhammānussatikathā..meaning: the discourse section on recollection of the Dhamma
breakdown: Recollection, repeated remembrance (anu + sati). Sandhi: dhamma + anu- > dhammānu-.

dhammānuvattino.meaning: following the Dhamma accordingly; those who conform to the Dhamma
dhammānuvattino.gram: compound adjective, masculine plural or genitive singular
breakdown: Following accordingly, conforming to (anu + √vat, to proceed). Sandhi: dhamma + anu- > dhammānu-.

dhammāsanāgato.meaning: gone to the Dhamma seat; seated on the teacher's seat
dhammāsanāgato.gram: compound adjective (past participle), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Gone (past participle of gacchati). Sandhi: āsana + āgato/gato > āsanāgato.

dhanahārakā.meaning: taking wealth; carriers or takers of wealth
breakdown: Taking, carrying off (from harati, to carry).
Bought (past participle of kiṇāti, to buy; the doubled kk is sandhi doubling).

dhanamanuppadeyyāsi.meaning: you should hand out wealth; you should provide property
dhanamanuppadeyyāsi.gram: verb phrase fused by sandhi; accusative noun + 2nd person singular optative of anuppadeti
breakdown: You should hand out, give over (optative of anuppadeti: anu + pa + √dā).

dhanatthaddho.meaning: selfish with wealth; hardened by riches
breakdown: Hard, rigid, selfish (past participle sense from √thambh).

dhañña.summary: Appears in the precept phrase āmakadhaññapaṭiggahaṇā (abstaining from accepting raw grain).
dhañña.gram: neuter noun / adjective, from dhana (√dhan + ya)
breakdown: Suffix; dhan + ya > dhañña, grain as wealth.

dhitimantānaṁ.meaning: of the firm, courageous, resolute ones

dhunanti.meaning: they shake; they shake off, destroy
breakdown: Root meaning to shake, shake off.
Present stem dhunā- with 3rd person plural ending.

dhurandhare.meaning: bearing a burden; being a supportive protector
dhurandhare.gram: compound adjective, locative singular or masculine plural
breakdown: Bearing, holding (√dhar); -n- is a junction consonant.

dhuravā.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular, from dhura

breakdown: Root meaning to hold, bear; dhur-, that which is borne.

dhutassa.meaning: of the ascetic practice; of one who has shaken off (defilements)
dhutassa.gram: neuter noun (past participle of dhunāti, √dhū), genitive/dative singular
breakdown: Root meaning to shake, shake off.
Past participle suffix: shaken off; by extension, the ascetic scrupulous practice.

dhutassa pāsādikassa.meaning: of the ascetic practice that inspires confidence; of scrupulous, gracious conduct
breakdown: Of the ascetic practice, of the shaking-off of defilements (√dhū + ta).
Of that which inspires confidence, gracious, pleasing (from pasāda, serene confidence).

dhutavadānaṁ.meaning: of those who punctiliously follow the ascetic practice; of the advocates of the dhuta practices
breakdown: The ascetic practice; shaken off (√dhū + ta).
Speaking of, advocating; genitive plural: of those who profess.

dhutaṅgasamādānaṁ.meaning: the taking up of the ascetic practices
breakdown: The ascetic practices (dhuta, shaking off + aṅga, factor): optional austere observances.
Root meaning to be fixed, constant.

dhāraṇa.gram: neuter action noun, in compounds, from dhāreti (√dhar)
breakdown: Stem of dhāreti, to hold, bear, wear.
Action noun suffix: the act of wearing, holding.

dhārenti.meaning: they take up; they hold, grasp; they wear
dhārenti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of dhāreti (√dhar)
breakdown: Stem of dhāreti, to hold, bear.

dhāressanti.meaning: they will bring; they will hold, bear
dhāressanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural future of dhāreti
breakdown: Stem of dhāreti, to hold, bear.

dhāreti.meaning: bears in mind; holds up; keeps; wears
breakdown: Causative-form present stem with 3rd person singular ending.

dhātusova.meaning: just by their nature; precisely according to their condition
dhātusova.gram: indeclinable adverb (ablative of dhātu) + emphatic particle, fused by sandhi
breakdown: By way of element, by their condition (dhātu, element + distributive -so).
Just, precisely (emphatic). Sandhi: dhātuso + eva > dhātusova.

dhātā.meaning: kept in mind; learned by heart; remembered
dhātā.gram: past participle of dhāreti (√dhar), plural or feminine form
breakdown: Stem of dhāreti, to hold, bear in mind.

dhātānaṁ.meaning: of those taken up; of what has been retained (in mind)
dhātānaṁ.gram: past participle of dhāreti, genitive/dative plural
breakdown: Held, taken up, retained (past participle of dhāreti).

dinnapāṭikaṅkhī.meaning: hoping for what is given; expecting only what is offered
breakdown: What is given (past participle of dadāti, √dā).
What is given (past participle of dadāti, √dā).
Taking, accepting (ā + √dā). Sandhi: dinna + ādāyī > dinnādāyī.

dinnāni.gram: past participle of dadāti (√dā), neuter nominative/accusative plural

breakdown: Present stem dippa- with 3rd person singular ending.
Archaic absolutive ending: having seen (dis + tvāna > disvāna).

diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ.meaning: of pleasant dwellings in this very life; of happy abidings here and now
breakdown: State, condition; diṭṭhadhamma = the seen state, this very life.

diṭṭhapadassa.meaning: of one who has seen the state (nibbāna); of one who has attained vision of the goal
breakdown: State, footing, track; here the deathless state, nibbāna.

diṭṭhe.meaning: in the seen; visible (in the phrase diṭṭhe dhamme, in this very life)

diṭṭhe dhamma.meaning: in the visible state; in this very world, here and now
diṭṭhe dhamma.gram: phrase; locative past participle + noun (diṭṭhe dhamme)
breakdown: State, condition; the phrase means the state determined by sight — this present life.

diṭṭhevadhamme.meaning: even in this present life; right here and now
diṭṭhevadhamme.gram: sandhi fusion: past participle (locative) + emphatic particle + masculine noun (locative)
breakdown: Even, just (emphatic). Sandhi: diṭṭhe + eva > diṭṭheva.
State, condition (locative): in this visible state, this very life.

diṭṭhi.meaning: view; opinion; belief (often wrong view, when unqualified)

diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā.meaning: by approval of a view after contemplation; through forbearance with a pondered opinion
breakdown: Acceptance, forbearance (the doubled kk is sandhi doubling).

diṭṭhā.gram: past participle of √dis, plural or feminine form
breakdown: Root meaning to see; dis + ta > diṭṭha.
